All other details remain unchanged.
The full amended text is shown below.
06 March 2026
AIM: SYN
Cambay PSC Production Update - Correction
Synergia Energy Limited (
"Synergia"
or the
"Company"
), announces the following correction to the RNS of 6.3.26 regarding the production update on its Cambay PSC (WI: 50%), onshore India.
Whereas the stated oil production is correct with the C-64 and C-74 wells having produced at a March-to-date average of 195 BOPD, the C-77H well is producing on plateau at circa
50,000
scfd.

latest.title: Cambay PSC Production Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 5656V
Synergia Energy Ltd
06 March 2026
RNS Announcement
06 March 2026
AIM: SYN
Cambay PSC Production Update
Synergia Energy Limited (
"Synergia"
or the
"Company"
), announces the following update regarding its Cambay PSC (WI: 50%), onshore India.
Production from the Cambay field saw a marked improvement from two legacy wells (C-64 and C-74) that were worked over in November 2025. Combined production from the C-64 and C-74 wells averaged 78 BOPD during February. Following recent increases in pump rates, the two wells have produced a March-to-date average of 195 BOPD. The C-77H gas well continues to produce on plateau at circa 500,000 scfd.
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR") and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.

latest.title: Cambay PSC Sale Agreement Update
latest.content:
Today 13:29
RNS Number : 2872S
Synergia Energy Ltd
09 February 2026
RNS Announcement
09 February 2026
AIM: SYN
Cambay PSC Sale Agreement Update
Synergia Energy Limited (
"Synergia"
or the
"Company"
), announces the following update regarding its Cambay PSC (WI: 50%), onshore India.
On 1 December 2025 Synergia and Antelopus Selan Energy Limited ("
Selan
") signed a Sale and Purchase Agreement ("
SPA
") on terms previously announced, with a total consideration of up to US$14 million payable in two tranches. The SPA was conditional on Synergia shareholder approval and Selan establishing a bank guarantee for the deferred payment tranche. Synergia shareholder approval was gained at the 29 December 2025 Synergia AGM.
Selan has failed to provide the required bank guarantee for the deferred tranche citing refusal by Selan's major shareholder, Oak Tree Capital, to sanction completion of the SPA.
Under the Heads of Terms signed by both parties on 4 July 2025, Selan had exclusivity until 8 February 2026. Synergia remains willing to complete the SPA, however, since the conditions precedent for the SPA have not been satisfied by Selan prior to 8 February 2026, Synergia intends to examine its options. In the event that the SPA is not completed, Selan's non-refundable payment of US $0.5 million made under the Heads of Terms will not be returned.
Roland Wessel, Synergia's CEO stated:
"
After 9 months of protracted negotiations, Selan has not satisfied the conditions precedent within the exclusivity period to complete
the transaction. We believe in the inherent value of the Cambay PSC's 206 BCF of proven P50 Eocene gas reserves. The parties remain bound by their obligations under the previously executed Farm-In/Farm-Out Agreement including the agreed work program and in particular the drilling of three new wells in the Eocene."
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR") and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.
For and on behalf of Synergia Energy Limited
Roland Wessel

latest.title: Cambay PSC Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 8566P
Synergia Energy Ltd
22 January 2026
RNS Announcement
22 January 2026
AIM: SYN
Cambay PSC Update
Synergia Energy Limited (
"Synergia"
or the
"Company"
), announces the following update regarding its Cambay PSC (WI: 50%), onshore India.
Cambay C78 Well Status:
Subsequent to the drilling and casing operations, the C-78 well was perforated over a 5 meter interval in the Oligocene OSII zone. The well showed gas during clean up and testing operations. The well is currently under observation to monitor pressure behaviour, which will help in subsequent technical studies and operational planning for future gas monetisation from C-78.
Workover Operations:
As a result of the workover operations, production has stabilised at an aggregate level of circa 40 bopd (gross) from the C-64 and C-74 wells.
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR") and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.

latest.title: Results of General Meeting
latest.content:
29 Dec 2025 11:22
RNS Number : 0003N
Synergia Energy Ltd
29 December 2025
For a printer friendly copy of this announcement, please click on the link below to open a PDF version.
http://www.rns-pdf.londonstockexchange.com/rns/0003N_1-2025-12-29.pdf
RNS Announcement
29 December 2025
AIM: SYN
Results of General Meeting
Synergia Energy Ltd (the
"
Company"
) is pleased to announce the voting results from the Company's General Meeting of Shareholders, held on Monday, 29 December 2025.
The Company advises that all motions put to the meeting were voted on by poll, with resolution one carried, resolution two was not passed. Details of the proxy voting and voting outcomes for all motions put to the meeting are set out in the attached report which can be accessed through the pdf link above.
Accordingly, and as previously advised, the Company will execute the Sale and Purchase Agreement and submit a Deed of Assignment to the Government of India to transfer the Company's 50% working interest in the Cambay PSC to Selan.
The Transaction remains subject to approval from the Government of India and subject to Selan establishing a bank guarantee for the deferred payment in a format suitable for the Company.
Following the Disposal,
The Company will re-focus its activities and will:
·
strive to progress the UK Medway Hub Camelot CCS project and install a new joint venture partner to replace Harbour Energy on the CS019 Camelot CCS licence;
·
seek to manage the return of capital to shareholders via a share buy-back scheme;
·
the India Project Office will manage the exit from the Cambay PSC and recovery of proceeds and withholding taxes;
·
progress the Cambay Basin CCS Scheme with regulatory authorities and the Government of India; and
·
seek to identify additional oil and gas development opportunities, primarily in India leveraging off the experience and expertise of the Company's management team.
In accordance with the result of the shareholder vote, the Company will no longer proceed with the planned delisting of the Company's shares on AIM at the present time and will focus on developing the Company's business and returning value to shareholders while remaining an AIM traded company.
Further announcements will be made as appropriate.
For and on behalf of Synergia Energy Ltd
Allison Pacinotti
Company Secretary
For further information, please contact:

company_30s.what_they_do: SYN.L is a small oil‑and‑gas exploration and production company focused on developing the Cambay oil field in India.  Its core activity is drilling and producing hydrocarbons from that field, with plans to expand into nearby blocks.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The Cambay field is one of the largest undeveloped assets in India, and its development would add significant supply to a market that is still tight on domestic production.  For investors, the company represents a high‑risk play with the potential for a large upside if the field is successfully commercialised.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in a lean, cash‑constrained phase.  It has recently filed an asset sale update, completed an annual report, and is preparing for a general meeting to discuss a proposed sale and changes to its governance.  The share price is trading near the lower end of its 52‑week range, indicating a steep decline from its all‑time high.

rns_narrative: SYN.L has been busy with corporate governance: the AGM, general meeting and major‑holding notifications show a focus on shareholder alignment and potential changes in ownership structure.
Financial reporting has been regular, with an audited annual report and an interim statement that highlight modest cash flow but significant debt and a need for capital.
The most headline‑making item is the Cambay PSC sale agreement update, signalling that the company is actively looking to exit or monetise its core asset.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Bullish chatter about a possible sale and production gains amid a quiet market.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Several community members highlight the 35% production jump reported by the company and the sale agreement, suggesting that a deal could unlock value and bring new capital.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Some voices warn that the company’s transparency is lacking and that the sale could stall, leaving the company still trapped in a cash‑tight situation with no clear exit.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has moved from cautious optimism to a more hopeful stance after the sale update, but overall engagement remains low, keeping the crowd largely silent.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for SYN.L is flat and dead, meaning the market is not currently tracking the stock.  This lack of attention is a classic contrarian signal for a potential surprise.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the Cambay PSC sale agreement – a formal announcement that the sale has closed or is near closing.
Publication of updated production data showing a sustained 35% increase or higher, which would validate the company’s operational claims.
A regulatory approval or a new drilling contract that signals the field is ready for commercial production.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in trading volume that exceeds twice the 30‑day average, a price break above the 0.01 support level, and a press release confirming the sale or new production figures.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early phase of a deep capitulation.  A strong catalyst could trigger a mean‑reversion move, but the recovery will likely be gradual given the company’s cash constraints and the need for a major transaction to provide liquidity.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company successfully finalises the Cambay sale, the share price could jump 100‑200% to reach the midpoint of its previous high.  The deep crash gives a 90%+ upside potential, while the risk is limited to the current price level.  Historical deep‑crash signals have shown that a 70‑95% fall can be followed by a dramatic rebound.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the sale stalls or the field’s production fails to meet expectations, the company could stay in a cash‑tight position, pushing the share price further down or even leading to a delisting.  The worst‑case loss is essentially the current price, but the probability of a total collapse is higher than in a more stable firm.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 100%+ if the share rebounds to the midpoint of its prior high.  Potential downside: 0% (current price) if the company fails to execute the sale or improve production.  The asymmetry is therefore highly favourable for a contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a volume spike that clears 2× the 30‑day average and a price break above the 0.01 support level.  Size the position at 5–10% of the portfolio, set a stop 15% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash or a small position until a formal sale announcement or new production data is released.  Trigger entry if the share price closes above 0.012 with volume above the 30‑day average.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risks identified.

ai_insights.bottom_line: SYN.L sits on a deep crash, a potentially game‑changing sale of its Cambay asset is on the horizon, and the market’s silence creates a high‑asymmetry contrarian opportunity.
